ok
1203 passed, 1 skipped in 25.51s

findings.summary: Every fired alert enqueued a FULL poll sweep of all orchestrator connections — Azure Monitor fires one webhook per pipeline dimension, so a batch failure amplifies into N simultaneous full sweeps (KV reads + ARM token grants per connection).
findings.failure_scenario: Bad deploy fails N pipelines → N fired webhooks in a minute → N full sweeps → external-API/KV amplification and worker-queue flood at the worst moment.

findings.summary: The ack claimed 'reconciling' even when the broker was down and no poll was enqueued (request_immediate_poll swallowed the exception).
findings.failure_scenario: Redis down when the alert arrives → Azure Monitor gets 200 'reconciling' but nothing was enqueued; run not recorded until the next 10-min beat.

findings.summary: Dimension scan had no break and last-match-wins: a later empty 'Name' dimension clobbered an already-found pipeline back to None.
findings.failure_scenario: Payload with dimensions [{Name: pl_orders}, {name: ""}] → pipeline_or_dag_id=None despite a valid value, degrading targeting + ack log.

findings.summary: Stale provisioning note said live ADF delivery 'still needs the Common-Alert-Schema mapping (#492)' — flatly wrong post-merge — and the guide didn't say the Action Group's common-alert-schema toggle must be ON.
findings.failure_scenario: Operator reads the guide, believes #492 is still open, defers the alert-rule setup or leaves the toggle off (legacy body → 422).

findings.summary: Dead double provider resolution: routes resolved the provider then _ack_event re-resolved it by name (unused in the AlertPing branch).
findings.failure_scenario: Two resolution sites that can drift + a dead registry lookup per event.

findings.summary: _parse_common_alert re-indexed body['data'] instead of the bound local, safe only via coupling to the essentials guard above.
findings.failure_scenario: A future refactor of the essentials check silently turns this into a TypeError on non-dict data.

result.output: 6 findings reported.

input.command:
git add -A backend/ deploy/README.md docs/orchestration.md && git commit -q -m "fixup: /code-review findings — targeted poll-now, honest ack, parse nits, docs

- poll_orchestration_runs now accepts provider/resource_name and the alert
  ping dispatches a TARGETED poll (this provider + the alerting factory via
  the resource_config_key seam) — an alert storm (one webhook per pipeline
  dimension) can no longer amplify into repeated full sweeps of every
  orchestrator connection. Beat path unchanged (no args = full sweep).
- request_immediate_poll returns whether the enqueue succeeded; the ack is
  honest: 'reconciling' only when the poll is actually queued, else 'ignored'
  (still 200 — never a retry-storm error, ADR 0006).
- _ack_event takes the already-resolved provider_impl (no dead re-resolution).
- _parse_common_alert: first non-empty 'Name' dimension wins (no clobber),
  data local reused, essentials narrowing made explicit.
- deploy/README step 5 + docs/orchestration.md: drop the stale '#492 still
  pending' note; document that the Action Group must enable the common alert
  schema and describe the poll-now behaviour.
- tests: targeted-poll narrowing matrix (worker), kwargs-asserting route test,
  broker-down honest-ack test; _capture stub widened.

.saveAsTable(f"{catalog}.silver.sales")

# COMMAND ----------
# GOLD — daily revenue rollup. Reconciles back to Snowflake order_total (ADR 0014):
# sum(gold.daily_revenue) should tie out to the Snowflake orders total — the
# cross-platform `comparison` target (built post-v1, shaped now).
(
    spark.table(f"{catalog}.silver.sales")
    .groupBy("sale_date", "channel")
    .agg(
        F.sum("amount").alias("daily_revenue"),
        F.countDistinct("order_number").alias("orders"),
    )
    .write.mode("overwrite")
    .saveAsTable(f"{catalog}.gold.daily_revenue")
)
    """Datasource-shaped run target (#215) — which table / flat-file path / Unity
    Catalog name the suite's checks run against. Same shape as the column-profiler
    request; `run_target.resolve_target` validates the right fields per connection
    type (`table` for SQL, `path` for flat files, `catalog` for Unity Catalog).

    A flat-file target can instead select a **batch** of files: `pattern` (a regex
    whose first capture group is the batch key) + `strategy` (`latest`/`specific`,
    with `batch` for `specific`) + optional `prefix` (A4). The exact field
    combination is validated by `run_target.resolve_target` per connection type, so
    those rules live in one place — this model only declares the storable keys."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True)

    table: str | None = Field(default=None, max_length=255)
    schema_: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="schema", max_length=255)
    catalog: str | None = Field(default=None, max_length=255)
    path: str | None = Field(default=None, max_length=1024)
    file_format: Literal["csv", "parquet"] | None = None
    # Flat-file batch selection (A4); validated in run_target, not here.
    pattern: str | None = Field(default=None, max_length=1024)
    strategy: Literal["latest", "specific"] | None = None
    batch: str | None = Field(default=None, max_length=255)
    prefix: str | None = Field(default=None, max_length=1024)

    def to_storage(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        """JSONB dict with the canonical `schema` key (not the `schema_` alias)."""
        return self.model_dump(by_alias=True, exclude_none=True)

def _run_suite(session: Session, *, run_id: uuid.UUID) -> str:
    """Load the run's graph, resolve its target, build the runner, execute.

    The suite's datasource-shaped ``target`` (#215) resolves to the runner's
    ``(table, schema, catalog)`` via ``run_target.resolve_target``; dispatch by
    ``connection.type`` through the runner registry gives a Snowflake / Unity
    Catalog / flat-file suite its correct `CheckRunner` (#146). A flat-file *batch*
    target is then materialized to a concrete path by listing the store
    (`materialize_path`).

    Failures while loading, resolving the target (targetless or malformed suite),
    or building the runner (missing rows, bad connection config, unresolved
    secret) drive the run to ``failed`` so it never lingers in ``queued``;
    execution failures are handled inside ``execute_run``. A genuinely-absent
    batch (`BatchNotFoundError`) is **not** a failure — the data hasn't landed, so
    every check is ``skip``ped (#122) and the run succeeds.
    """
    run = session.get(Run, run_id)
    if run is None:
        log.error("run_suite_run_not_found", run_id=str(run_id))
        return "not_found"

    # Cooperative cancellation: a cancel that landed while the run was queued (or
    # in the dispatch→pickup window) already set 'cancelled' — don't execute it.
    # (revoke also drops a still-queued task; this is the belt-and-braces check.)
    if run.status == "cancelled":
        log.info("run_suite_already_cancelled", run_id=str(run_id))
        return "cancelled"

    try:
        suite = session.get(Suite, run.suite_id)
        connection = session.get(Connection, suite.connection_id) if suite is not None else None
        if suite is None or connection is None:
            raise RuntimeError("suite or connection not found for run")
        target = run_target.resolve_target(connection.type, suite.target)
        checks = list(session.scalars(select(Check).where(Check.suite_id == suite.id)))
        runner = build_check_runner(
            conn_type=connection.type,
            config=connection.config,
            secret_ref=connection.secret_ref,
            secret_store=get_secret_store(),
            catalog=target.catalog,
        )
    except Exception:
        return _terminal_failed(session, run, event="run_suite_setup_failed", run_id=run_id)

    # Materialize the concrete path (live for a flat-file batch target). Kept
    # separate from setup so a missing batch is a skip, not a setup failure.
    try:
        table = run_target.materialize_path(
            connection.type,
            connection.config,
            target,
            secret_ref=connection.secret_ref,
            secret_store=get_secret_store(),
        )
    except BatchNotFoundError:
        run_service.skip_run(session, run=run, checks=checks, reason="batch_not_found")
        log.info("run_suite_skipped_no_batch", run_id=str(run_id), suite_id=str(suite.id))
        return str(run.status)
    except Exception:
        return _terminal_failed(session, run, event="run_suite_materialize_failed", run_id=run_id)

    # The suite's identifier column (#415) — requested from GX so failing rows are
    # captured with a locator. A `None`/absent policy keeps the scalar-only sample.
    policy = suite.column_policy or {}
    identifier = policy.get("identifier_column")
    index_columns = [str(identifier)] if identifier else None

    run_service.execute_run(
        session,
        run=run,
        checks=checks,
        runner=runner,

input.command:
gh issue create --title "fix(deps): UC runs broken in prod — databricks-sql-connector 4.x no longer bundles the SQLAlchemy dialect" --label "bug" --milestone "Week 7 — Deployment, hardening & docs" --body "Found by the Week-7 **live smoke** (deferred live warehouse/file run): a UC suite run against the harness (\`dataq_retail.gold.daily_revenue\`) fails in prod with

\`\`\`
NoSuchModuleError: Can't load plugin: sqlalchemy.dialects:databricks
\`\`\`

at \`backend/app/datasources/unity_catalog.py:147\` (\`_read_table\` → \`create_engine('databricks://…')\`).

## Root cause
The #129 CVE refresh bumped \`databricks-sql-connector\` 3→4. In 4.x the SQLAlchemy dialect was **split out** into the separate \`databricks-sqlalchemy\` package, so the \`sqlalchemy.dialects:databricks\` entry point no longer exists in our image. Local/CI tests mock the runner seam (no live UC creds), so nothing caught it — precisely the gap the live smoke exists to catch (CONTRIBUTING 4a: don't mock the seam you're validating).

## Fix
- Pin \`databricks-sqlalchemy\` in \`backend/requirements.txt\` next to the connector.
- Note the pairing in the connector's comment so the next major bump checks it.

## Evidence
Run \`f424b652\` (suite \"Live smoke — UC gold\") failed 2026-07-02T03:13:54Z; worker traceback in App Insights. The operational-failure alert (Slack+email) fired correctly (#419)." -q ".url" 2>/dev/null || gh issue list --limit 1 --json number,title -q '.[0]'

input.command:
gh issue create --title "fix(deps): UC runs broken in prod — databricks-sql-connector 4.x no longer bundles the SQLAlchemy dialect" --label "bug" --milestone "Week 7 — Deployment, hardening & docs" --body "Found by the Week-7 **live smoke** (deferred live warehouse/file run): a UC suite run against the harness (\`dataq_retail.gold.daily_revenue\`) fails in prod with

\`\`\`
NoSuchModuleError: Can't load plugin: sqlalchemy.dialects:databricks
\`\`\`

at \`backend/app/datasources/unity_catalog.py:147\` (\`_read_table\` → \`create_engine('databricks://…')\`).

## Root cause
The #129 CVE refresh bumped \`databricks-sql-connector\` 3→4. In 4.x the SQLAlchemy dialect was **split out** into the separate \`databricks-sqlalchemy\` package, so the \`sqlalchemy.dialects:databricks\` entry point no longer exists in our image. Local/CI tests mock the runner seam (no live UC creds), so nothing caught it — precisely the gap the live smoke exists to catch (CONTRIBUTING 4a).

## Fix
- Pin \`databricks-sqlalchemy\` in \`backend/requirements.txt\` next to the connector; note the pairing in the connector's comment.

## Evidence
Run \`f424b652\` (suite \"Live smoke — UC gold\") failed 2026-07-02T03:13:54Z; worker traceback in App Insights. The operational-failure alert (Slack+email) fired correctly (#419)."; gh issue create --title "fix(logging): exception tracebacks capture frame locals — connection URLs with embedded credentials reach the logs" --label "bug" --milestone "Week 7 — Deployment, hardening & docs" --body "Found during the Week-7 live smoke. \`structlog.processors.dict_tracebacks\` defaults to \`ExceptionDictTransformer(show_locals=True)\`, so a runner exception serializes **frame locals** into the log event. A SQLAlchemy engine URL local (\`<scheme>://token:<credential>@host/…\`) rides along verbatim — the PII redactor's key-based pass and the \`_SECRET_QS_RE\` string scrub (\`token=…\` query-param shape, #494) both miss **URL-userinfo** credentials (\`user:secret@\`), and miss locals values generally.

## Fix (both layers)
1. Configure the traceback dict transform with \`show_locals=False\` — locals are debugging sugar; they can carry anything (credentials, sample rows/PII) and are unredactable in general.
2. Defense-in-depth: extend \`_scrub_secret_strings\` with a URL-userinfo pattern (\`://<user>:<secret>@\` → \`://<user>:<redacted>@\`).

## Operational follow-up
Rotate the credential that reached the logs (coordinated separately, not named here).

Refs: #494 (query-string leak class), \`backend/app/core/logging.py\`."

def test_databricks_sqlalchemy_dialect_is_installed() -> None:
    """Dependency contract (#535): `_read_table` does
    `create_engine('databricks://…')`, whose dialect lives in the separate
    `databricks-sqlalchemy` package since databricks-sql-connector 4.x —
    tests mock the runner seam, so without this check a missing dialect only
    surfaces as a failed run in production. No network: dialect load only.
    """
    from sqlalchemy import create_engine

    engine = create_engine(
        "REDACTED"
        "?http_path=/sql/1.0/warehouses/x&catalog=c"
    )
    assert engine.dialect.name == "databricks"
EOF
conda run -n dataq black -q backend/tests/datasources/test_unity_catalog.py && conda run -n dataq python -m pytest backend/tests/datasources/test_unity_catalog.py -q 2>&1 | tail -1 && conda run -n dataq pip-audit -r backend/requirements-dev.txt 2>&1 | tail -1

git add backend/requirements.txt backend/tests/datasources/test_unity_catalog.py && git commit -q -m "fix(deps): restore the SQLAlchemy databricks dialect — UC runs were broken in prod

databricks-sql-connector 4.x (the #129 CVE bump) split the SQLAlchemy dialect
into the separate databricks-sqlalchemy package, so create_engine('databricks://')
raised NoSuchModuleError in the prod image and every Unity Catalog suite run
failed operationally. Caught by the Week-7 live smoke (run f424b652); CI never
saw it because tests mock the runner seam (CONTRIBUTING 4a lesson, again).

- pin databricks-sqlalchemy==2.0.9 next to the connector, with a bump-the-pair
  comment
- dependency-contract test: the 'databricks' dialect must load (no network)

Fixes #535

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>" && git push -qu origin fix/issue-535-databricks-sqlalchemy-dialect 2>&1 | tail -1 && gh pr create --title "fix(deps): restore the SQLAlchemy databricks dialect (UC runs broken in prod)" --milestone "Week 7 — Deployment, hardening & docs" --body "## Summary
**Fixes #535** — found live by the Week-7 smoke: every UC suite run failed with \`NoSuchModuleError: Can't load plugin: sqlalchemy.dialects:databricks\` because the #129 bump to \`databricks-sql-connector\` 4.x silently dropped the bundled SQLAlchemy dialect (now the separate \`databricks-sqlalchemy\` package).

- Pin \`databricks-sqlalchemy==2.0.9\` (bump-the-pair comment on the connector line).
- Dependency-contract test: \`create_engine('databricks://…')\` must resolve the dialect — no network, closes the mocked-seam blind spot for this class of unbundling.

## Test plan
- New test fails on main, passes here; full \`pip-audit\` clean.
- Live re-verification of the UC run after deploy (part of the in-flight live-smoke session).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)"

input.new_string:
# Secret-bearing query params / `key=value` pairs embedded in *string* values
# (the key-based redaction below only catches dict KEYS). The prime case is the
# ADF webhook URL `…/events/adf?token=<secret>` (ADR 0006) surfacing inside a log
# message string — e.g. an access line or an error that interpolated the URL —
# where it would otherwise slip past the key redactor (#494).
_SECRET_QS_RE = re.compile(
    r"(?i)\b(token|sig|signature|secret|api[_-]?key|access[_-]?key|password)=[^&\s\"']+"
)
# URL-userinfo credentials (`REDACTED`, e.g. a SQLAlchemy engine
# URL `databricks://token:<PAT>@host/…`) — a different shape than the query-param
# scrub above, missed by it until #536.
_URL_USERINFO_RE = re.compile(r"(?i)\b([a-z][a-z0-9+.-]*://[^/\s:@\"']+):[^@/\s\"']+@")


def _scrub_secret_strings(text: str) -> str:
    text = _SECRET_QS_RE.sub(lambda m: f"{m.group(1)}={_REDACTED}", text)
    return _URL_USERINFO_RE.sub(lambda m: f"{m.group(1)}:{_REDACTED}@", text)

input.new_string:
# Traceback → dict WITHOUT frame locals (#536): `dict_tracebacks`' default
# transformer captures every frame's locals, which can carry anything in scope —
# connection URLs with embedded credentials (the live-smoke leak: a SQLAlchemy
# `…://token:<PAT>@host` engine URL), sample rows, PII — and are unredactable in
# general. Frame files/lines/names remain; locals are debugging sugar we forgo.
_dict_tracebacks_no_locals = structlog.processors.ExceptionRenderer(
    structlog.tracebacks.ExceptionDictTransformer(show_locals=False)
)


def configure_logging() -> None:

"""Tests for the PII redactor (post-2026-05-28 security audit additions)."""

import logging as std_logging
from collections.abc import Iterator

import pytest

from backend.app.core.config import get_settings
from backend.app.core.logging import _redact_pii, configure_logging


def _redact(payload: dict[str, object]) -> dict[str, object]:
    """Apply the redactor as a free function (mirrors structlog processor invocation)."""
    return dict(_redact_pii(None, "", dict(payload)))


def test_redacts_credentials_and_personal_contact() -> None:
    out = _redact(
        {
            "event": "auth_attempt",
def test_scrubs_secret_query_params_in_string_values() -> None:
    """#494: a token embedded in a message STRING (e.g. the ADF webhook URL) must be
    scrubbed — the key-based redaction only catches dict keys."""
    out = _redact(
        {"event": 'POST /api/v1/orchestration/events/adf?token=s3cr3t-VALUE.1 HTTP/1.1" 200'}
    )
    assert "s3cr3t-VALUE.1" not in str(out["event"])
    assert "token=<redacted>" in str(out["event"])


def test_scrubs_assorted_secret_params_but_keeps_safe_pairs() -> None:
    out = _redact({"event": "https://h/x?api_key=AAA&signature=BBB&page=2"})
    assert "AAA" not in str(out["event"]) and "BBB" not in str(out["event"])
    assert "api_key=<redacted>" in str(out["event"])
    assert "signature=<redacted>" in str(out["event"])
    assert "page=2" in str(out["event"])  # non-secret param untouched

def test_scrubs_url_userinfo_credentials() -> None:
    """#536: a SQLAlchemy-style engine URL carries the credential in the URL
    USERINFO (`REDACTED`), not a query param — the #494 regex
    missed that shape entirely."""
    out = _redact(
        {
            "event": "engine url REDACTED"
            "?http_path=/sql/1.0/warehouses/x"
        }
    )
    assert "dapiDEADBEEF123" not in str(out["event"])
    assert "databricks://token:<redacted>@dbc-x.cloud.databricks.com" in str(out["event"])
    # Non-credential URLs are untouched.
    out2 = _redact({"event": "see https://docs.example.com/path and postgres://host/db"})
    assert out2["event"] == "see https://docs.example.com/path and postgres://host/db"


def test_exception_tracebacks_drop_frame_locals_and_scrub_messages(
    capsys: pytest.CaptureFixture[str],
) -> None:
    """#536: frame locals (which can hold anything in scope — engine URLs with
    embedded credentials, sample rows) must NOT be serialized into the log
    event, and the rendered exception strings pass the scrubber."""
    configure_logging()
    logger = std_logging.getLogger("test.locals.leak")
    try:
        engine_url = "REDACTED"  # the leaking local
        raise RuntimeError(f"connect failed for REDACTED ({len(engine_url)})")
    except RuntimeError:
        logger.exception("boom")
    line = capsys.readouterr().out
    assert "dapiSHOULDNOTLEAK" not in line  # locals not captured at all
    assert '"locals"' not in line
    assert "dapiALSONOT" not in line  # exception MESSAGE passed the scrubber
    assert "token:<redacted>@" in line
    assert "RuntimeError" in line  # the traceback itself is still there
EOF

input.command:
git add backend/app/core/logging.py backend/tests/core/test_logging.py && git commit -q -m "fix(logging): drop traceback frame locals + scrub URL-userinfo credentials

Found by the Week-7 live smoke: a runner exception serialized frame locals
into the log event (structlog dict_tracebacks defaults to show_locals=True),
carrying a SQLAlchemy engine URL with the credential in the URL userinfo
(scheme://token:<secret>@host) verbatim into stdout + App Insights. The #494
scrubber only matched the token=<...> query-param shape.

- Render tracebacks with ExceptionDictTransformer(show_locals=False): locals
  can hold anything in scope (credentials, sample rows/PII) and are
  unredactable in general; frame files/lines/names remain.
- _scrub_secret_strings: new URL-userinfo pattern (scheme://user:secret@ ->
  scheme://user:<redacted>@), defense-in-depth for exception MESSAGES.
- Re-run _redact_pii after traceback rendering in the formatter chain — the
  pre-chain pass ran before the exception existed as strings.
- Tests: userinfo scrub (+ non-credential URLs untouched); end-to-end
  formatter test proving locals are gone and exception messages are scrubbed.

Operational follow-up (out of repo): rotate the credential that reached logs.

Fixes #536

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>" && git push -qu origin fix/issue-536-traceback-locals-credential-leak 2>&1 | tail -1 && gh pr create --title "fix(logging): drop traceback frame locals + scrub URL-userinfo credentials" --milestone "Week 7 — Deployment, hardening & docs" --body "## Summary
**Fixes #536** — found live: a UC runner exception shipped **frame locals** (structlog \`dict_tracebacks\` defaults \`show_locals=True\`) to stdout + App Insights, including a SQLAlchemy engine URL with its credential in the **URL userinfo** — a shape the #494 query-param scrubber doesn't match.

Three layers:
1. Tracebacks render with \`show_locals=False\` — locals can hold anything in scope and are unredactable in general.
2. \`_scrub_secret_strings\` gains a \`REDACTED` → \`scheme://user:<redacted>@\` pattern (exception messages, any string field).
3. \`_redact_pii\` re-runs **after** traceback rendering in the formatter chain (the pre-chain pass ran before the exception existed as strings).

## Test plan
- New tests: userinfo scrub + non-credential URLs untouched; an end-to-end formatter test raising with a secret-bearing local — asserts no locals key, the local's secret absent, the exception-message secret scrubbed, traceback retained.
- Full suite 1193 passed; black/ruff/mypy/bandit green.
- Operational: the leaked credential is being rotated (coordinated outside the repo).

import json, httpx

BASE = "https://dataq-app-frontend.purplefield-f7322a1b.westus2.azurecontainerapps.io/mcp"
sess = json.load(open("frontend/e2e-live/.auth/session.json"))
tok = json.loads([v for k, v in sess.items() if k.startswith("oidc.user:")][0])["access_token"]
H = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {tok}", "Content-Type": "application/json",
     "Accept": "application/json, text/event-stream"}

def parse(r):
    if "text/event-stream" in r.headers.get("content-type", ""):
        for line in r.text.splitlines():
            if line.startswith("data:"):
                return json.loads(line[5:])
    return r.json() if r.text else None

c = httpx.Client(timeout=60)
r = c.post(BASE, headers=H, json={"jsonrpc": "2.0", "id": 1, "method": "initialize",
    "params": {"protocolVersion": "2025-06-18", "capabilities": {},
               "clientInfo": {"name": "dataq-live-smoke", "version": "1"}}})
print("initialize:", r.status_code)
sid = r.headers.get("mcp-session-id")
if sid: H["mcp-session-id"] = sid
c.post(BASE, headers=H, json={"jsonrpc": "2.0", "method": "notifications/initialized"})

r = c.post(BASE, headers=H, json={"jsonrpc": "2.0", "id": 2, "method": "tools/list"})
tools = [t["name"] for t in parse(r)["result"]["tools"]]
print("tools:", tools)

def call(i, name, args):
    r = c.post(BASE, headers=H, json={"jsonrpc": "2.0", "id": i, "method": "tools/call",
        "params": {"name": name, "arguments": args}})
    out = parse(r)
    res = out.get("result", out)
    txt = (res.get("content") or [{}])[0].get("text", "")[:220]
    print(f"\n[{name}] err={res.get('isError', False)}")
    print(" ", txt.replace("\n", " ")[:220])
    return res

# Q1 'what failed today?' -> health + suite results
call(3, "get_health_score", {"window_days": 1})
suites = call(4, "list_suites", {})
retail = json.loads((suites.get("content") or [{}])[0].get("text", "[]"))
rid = next((s["id"] for s in retail if "Retail" in s.get("name", "")), None)
call(5, "get_suite_results", {"suite_id": rid})
# Q2 'run the orders suite' -> trigger_suite_run
run = call(6, "trigger_suite_run", {"suite_id": rid})
# Q3 'why did the customer pipeline fail?' -> pipeline status
call(7, "get_adf_pipeline_status", {"provider": "adf", "limit": 5})
# Q4 'add a null check on email' -> create_check (on the temp UC suite, cleaned later)
call(8, "create_check", {"suite_id": "f63aac1c-1848-4f7f-a0c9-1a11dd5eb9fe",
     "name": "mcp smoke - email not null", "expectation_type": "expect_column_values_to_not_be_null",
     "config": {"column": "channel"}})
EOF

gh issue create --title "ci(deploy): frontend 'az containerapp update' fails with KeyError: 'properties' (2/2 runs) — manual retry succeeds" --label "bug" --milestone "Week 7 — Deployment, hardening & docs" --body "Both Deploy runs on 2026-07-02 (28561891145, 28563255383) failed the **Frontend — build image, deploy to Container App** job at the \`Deploy frontend\` step:

\`\`\`
ERROR: The command failed with an unexpected error. Here is the traceback:
ERROR: 'properties'
  File \".../containerapp/_client_factory.py\", line 28, in _polish_bad_errors
KeyError: 'properties'
\`\`\`

The same \`az containerapp update --image …\` retried manually from a workstation succeeds immediately, and the backend job's identical pattern (az containerapp update in \`deploy.yml\`) passes — so this looks like the runner's azure-cli containerapp extension version choking on an ARM error/response shape (\`_polish_bad_errors\` masking the real payload), possibly a transient 429/409 during the roll.

## Options
- Pin/upgrade the \`containerapp\` extension version on the runner before use (\`az extension add -n containerapp --version <known-good>\`), matching the backend job if it differs.
- Add one retry with backoff around the update step (the operation is idempotent).
- Surface the real error: run with \`--debug\` on failure so the masked response is visible.

Backend job is unaffected; both incidents left prod frontend on the previous tag until the manual retry." 2>&1 | tail -1

input.command:
gh issue create --title "fix(db): DELETE /suites/{id} 500s once the suite has run — results.check_id FK lacks ON DELETE" --label "bug" --milestone "Week 7 — Deployment, hardening & docs" --body "Found by the Week-7 live smoke while cleaning up its temp suites: deleting a suite whose checks have **result rows** 500s with

\`\`\`
psycopg2.errors.ForeignKeyViolation: update or delete on table \"checks\" violates
foreign key constraint \"fk_results_check_id_checks\" on table \"results\"
\`\`\`

\`results.check_id\` (models.py:357) is the **only FK in the schema without an \`ondelete\`** — suites→checks and runs→results both CASCADE, but the checks-side delete trips over surviving results rows (ordering-dependent). Net effect: **any suite that has ever run cannot be deleted from the UI/API**; E2E delete tests never caught it because their authoring-loop suites never ran.

## Fix
- \`ondelete=\"CASCADE\"\` on \`results.check_id\` (matches ADR 0020's accepted cascade-delete posture and the run_id FK).
- Backward-compatible Alembic migration (drop + recreate the constraint with the cascade; no column change).
- Regression test: delete a suite that has a run with results.

Deleting a RUN already cascades its results (run_id FK), so no retention change — this only fixes the delete path ordering." 2>&1 | tail -1; git checkout main -q && git pull -q && git checkout -b fix/issue-540-results-check-fk-cascade 2>&1 | tail -1

python3 - <<'EOF'
import os, re
revs, downs = {}, set()
for f in os.listdir("backend/alembic/versions"):
    if not f.endswith(".py"): continue
    s = open(f"backend/alembic/versions/{f}").read()
    r = re.search(r'^revision: str = "([^"]+)"', s, re.M)
    d = re.search(r'^down_revision: str \| None = "([^"]+)"', s, re.M)
    if r: revs[r.group(1)] = f
    if d: downs.add(d.group(1))
heads = [ (r, f) for r, f in revs.items() if r not in downs ]
print("HEAD:", heads)
EOF

input.description: Read latest migration for style

result.output:
"""downgrade legacy admin shares to edit (ADR 0027 / #482)

Revision ID: e5f6a7b8c9d0
Revises: d4e5f6a7b8c9
Create Date: 2026-06-30 02:00:00.000000+00:00

ADR 0027 removes grantable suite-``admin``: a normal user can only hold a
``view``/``edit`` share, and ``admin`` is now the *workspace-admin* (implicit on
every suite, never a ``shares`` row). The step-1 code stopped issuing new
``admin`` shares (the ``SharePermission`` Literal is ``view|edit`` and
``share_service`` rejects anything else) but deliberately kept reading any
pre-existing ``shares.permission = 'admin'`` rows as ``admin`` so nobody lost
access between the two deploys (backward-compatible).

This migration retires those legacy rows by downgrading them to ``edit`` — the
closest non-removed capability (they keep check-authoring/run access, lose
manage-shares/delete, which is now workspace-admin-only). After this runs there
should be no ``admin`` share rows; the new grant path already can't create more.

**Not a schema change.** The ``permission_valid`` CHECK still lists ``admin``
(``models.PERMISSIONS`` is unchanged) so the value stays legal at the DB layer —
this keeps a rollback to step-1 code safe and is harmless once the rows are gone.

**Tested up + down locally.** Down is a deliberate no-op: the original set of
``admin`` grantees is not recoverable (we can't tell which ``edit`` rows were
once ``admin``), and the affected users retain a valid ``edit`` share either way,
so there is no access regression to reverse. A rollback that genuinely needs the
old admins back must re-grant them out of band (and, pre-ADR-0027, via code that
still allowed it).
"""

from collections.abc import Sequence

from alembic import op

# revision identifiers, used by Alembic.
revision: str = "e5f6a7b8c9d0"
down_revision: str | None = "d4e5f6a7b8c9"
branch_labels: str | Sequence[str] | None = None
depends_on: str | Sequence[str] | None = None


def upgrade() -> None:
    op.execute("UPDATE shares SET permission = 'edit' WHERE permission = 'admin'")

input.content:
"""results.check_id -> ON DELETE CASCADE (#540)

Revision ID: a9b0c1d2e3f4
Revises: e5f6a7b8c9d0
Create Date: 2026-07-02 04:00:00.000000+00:00

``results.check_id`` was the only FK in the schema without an ``ondelete``, so
deleting a suite whose checks have result rows hit
``fk_results_check_id_checks`` (the suites→checks cascade fires while
runs→results rows still reference the checks) and the API 500'd — any suite
that had ever run could not be deleted. Found by the Week-7 live smoke.

CASCADE matches the schema's posture everywhere else (ADR 0020: cascade-delete
accepted; ``results.run_id`` already cascades), and changes nothing about
result retention outside the delete path: results already die with their run.

**Backward-compatible**: constraint drop + recreate only, no column change;
old code keeps working during a rolling deploy. **Tested up + down locally.**
Down restores the original NO ACTION constraint (rows deleted by a cascade in
the interim are gone, as accepted by ADR 0020).
"""

from collections.abc import Sequence

from alembic import op

# revision identifiers, used by Alembic.
revision: str = "a9b0c1d2e3f4"
down_revision: str | None = "e5f6a7b8c9d0"
branch_labels: str | Sequence[str] | None = None
depends_on: str | Sequence[str] | None = None

_FK = "fk_results_check_id_checks"


def upgrade() -> None:
    op.drop_constraint(_FK, "results", type_="foreignkey")
    op.create_foreign_key(
        _FK, "results", "checks", ["check_id"], ["id"], ondelete="CASCADE"
    )


def downgrade() -> None:
    op.drop_constraint(_FK, "results", type_="foreignkey")
    op.create_foreign_key(_FK, "results", "checks", ["check_id"], ["id"])

    check_id: Mapped[uuid.UUID] = mapped_column(

def test_delete_returns_204_then_404(client: TestClient, db_session: Any) -> None:
    conn = _connection(db_session)
    sid = client.post("/api/v1/suites", json=_payload(conn.id)).json()["id"]
    deleted = client.delete(f"/api/v1/suites/{sid}")
    assert deleted.status_code == 204
    assert client.get(f"/api/v1/suites/{sid}").status_code == 404


def test_delete_cascades_to_checks(client: TestClient, db_session: Any) -> None:
    conn = _connection(db_session)
    sid = client.post("/api/v1/suites", json=_payload(conn.id)).json()["id"]
    # attach a check directly (check CRUD lands in the next PR)
    db_session.add(
        Check(suite_id=uuid.UUID(sid), name="row_count", expectation_type="expect_table_row_count")
    )
    db_session.commit()

    deleted = client.delete(f"/api/v1/suites/{sid}")
    assert deleted.status_code == 204
    remaining = db_session.scalars(select(Check).where(Check.suite_id == uuid.UUID(sid))).all()
    assert remaining == []  # cascade removed the child check


# ───────────────────────── access enforcement (PR-E2) ──────────────
def client(db_session: Any) -> Iterator[TestClient]:
    app.dependency_overrides[get_db] = lambda: db_session
    try:
        yield TestClient(app)
    finally:
        app.dependency_overrides.clear()


def _connection(db_session: Any) -> Connection:
    """Insert a connection (with its own owner) for suites to bind to."""
    owner = User(aad_object_id=uuid.uuid4().hex, email="owner@example.com")
    db_session.add(owner)
    db_session.flush()
    conn = Connection(
        name=f"sf-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}",
        type="snowflake",
        env="dev",
        config={"account": "ab12345.eu-west-1"},
        secret_ref="kv-sf",
        created_by=owner.id,

input.old_string:
"""results.check_id -> ON DELETE CASCADE (#540)

Revision ID: a9b0c1d2e3f4
Revises: e5f6a7b8c9d0
Create Date: 2026-07-02 04:00:00.000000+00:00

``results.check_id`` was the only FK in the schema without an ``ondelete``, so
deleting a suite whose checks have result rows hit
``fk_results_check_id_checks`` (the suites→checks cascade fires while
runs→results rows still reference the checks) and the API 500'd — any suite
that had ever run could not be deleted. Found by the Week-7 live smoke.

CASCADE matches the schema's posture everywhere else (ADR 0020: cascade-delete
accepted; ``results.run_id`` already cascades), and changes nothing about
result retention outside the delete path: results already die with their run.

**Backward-compatible**: constraint drop + recreate only, no column change;
old code keeps working during a rolling deploy. **Tested up + down locally.**
Down restores the original NO ACTION constraint (rows deleted by a cascade in
the interim are gone, as accepted by ADR 0020).
"""

from collections.abc import Sequence

from alembic import op

# revision identifiers, used by Alembic.
revision: str = "a9b0c1d2e3f4"
down_revision: str | None = "e5f6a7b8c9d0"
branch_labels: str | Sequence[str] | None = None
depends_on: str | Sequence[str] | None = None

_FK = "fk_results_check_id_checks"


def upgrade() -> None:
    op.drop_constraint(_FK, "results", type_="foreignkey")
    op.create_foreign_key(_FK, "results", "checks", ["check_id"], ["id"], ondelete="CASCADE")


def downgrade() -> None:
    op.drop_constraint(_FK, "results", type_="foreignkey")
    op.create_foreign_key(_FK, "results", "checks", ["check_id"], ["id"])
input.new_string:
"""suite-delete cascades: results.check_id + runs.suite_id -> ON DELETE CASCADE (#540)

Revision ID: a9b0c1d2e3f4
Revises: e5f6a7b8c9d0
Create Date: 2026-07-02 04:00:00.000000+00:00

Deleting a suite that had ever RUN 500'd: the ORM cascades the suite's checks,
but ``results.check_id`` (and, next in line, ``runs.suite_id``) had **no
``ondelete``**, so the DB rejected the delete with a ForeignKeyViolation.
Found by the Week-7 live smoke cleaning up its temp suites.

CASCADE matches the schema's posture elsewhere (ADR 0020: cascade-delete
accepted; ``results.run_id`` / ``checks.suite_id`` already cascade) and
changes nothing about retention outside the delete path.

**Backward-compatible**: constraint drop + recreate only, no column change;
old code keeps working during a rolling deploy. **Tested up + down locally.**
Down restores the original NO ACTION constraints (rows deleted by a cascade
in the interim are gone, as accepted by ADR 0020).
"""

from collections.abc import Sequence

from alembic import op

# revision identifiers, used by Alembic.
revision: str = "a9b0c1d2e3f4"
down_revision: str | None = "e5f6a7b8c9d0"
branch_labels: str | Sequence[str] | None = None
depends_on: str | Sequence[str] | None = None

# (constraint name, table, referred table, column)
_FKS = [
    ("fk_results_check_id_checks", "results", "checks", "check_id"),
    ("fk_runs_suite_id_suites", "runs", "suites", "suite_id"),
]


def upgrade() -> None:
    for name, table, referred, column in _FKS:
        op.drop_constraint(name, table, type_="foreignkey")
        op.create_foreign_key(name, table, referred, [column], ["id"], ondelete="CASCADE")


def downgrade() -> None:
    for name, table, referred, column in _FKS:
        op.drop_constraint(name, table, type_="foreignkey")
        op.create_foreign_key(name, table, referred, [column], ["id"])

<result>## Migration review — `a9b0c1d2e3f4_results_check_fk_cascade.py`

Branch `fix/issue-540-results-check-fk-cascade` has no commits yet (`git log main..HEAD` is empty) — everything is uncommitted working-tree state: the new migration file (untracked) plus `backend/app/db/models.py` (modified). I reviewed the working tree directly since `main...HEAD` diffs nothing.

Files reviewed:
- `/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/backend/alembic/versions/a9b0c1d2e3f4_results_check_fk_cascade.py`
- `/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/backend/app/db/models.py` (diff)
- `/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/backend/app/db/base.py` (naming convention)
- `/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/backend/alembic/env.py` (transaction wrapping)
- `/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/backend/alembic/versions/923dab15eb8c_baseline.py` (constraint names at creation)
- `/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/backend/app/services/suite_service.py` (delete-suite path)

### 🔴 Hard violations
None of the enumerated hard-violation patterns apply. No column drop/rename/type-change, no nullable flip without a default, no `drop_table`/`rename_table`, no raw destructive SQL in `op.execute`, `downgrade()` is a real inverse (not `pass`), and `down_revision = "e5f6a7b8c9d0"` correctly points at the actual latest head (grep across all 17 revision files confirms nothing else claims that head — no stray merge).

### 🟡 Concerns

**1. Lock behavior on `results` (the item you specifically flagged) — real and worth a hard look before merge, file `a9b0c1d2e3f4_results_check_fk_cascade.py:41-42`.**

`op.drop_constraint(name, table, type_="foreignkey")` followed immediately by `op.create_foreign_key(..., ondelete="CASCADE")` runs inside **one Alembic transaction** — `backend/alembic/env.py` uses the default `context.begin_transaction()` with no `transaction_per_migration=False`/autocommit block. That matters because:

- `DROP CONSTRAINT` alone requires `ACCESS EXCLUSIVE` on the table, but it's fast (catalog-only, no scan).
- `ADD CONSTRAINT ... FOREIGN KEY` in isolation only requires `SHARE ROW EXCLUSIVE` (blocks writers, not readers) — **but** it also performs a full validation scan of the referencing table by default (no `NOT VALID`), and that scan runs under whatever lock is held.
- Postgres never *downgrades* a lock mid-transaction. Once the `DROP CONSTRAINT` statement has taken `ACCESS EXCLUSIVE` on `results`, that lock is held for the rest of the transaction — through the subsequent `ADD CONSTRAINT`'s full-table validation scan.

Net effect: for the duration it takes Postgres to scan the *entire* `results` table to re-validate the FK, `results` is `ACCESS EXCLUSIVE`-locked — **blocking every read and write**, not just writes. Any in-flight API request reading `/runs/{id}` results, or a worker writing a new `Result` row, will block for that window; if it exceeds a client/API `statement_timeout`, those calls fail. Given ADR 0024's migrate-job-then-roll deploy model, this happens while the *old* code image is still live and serving traffic — the failure mode isn't "crashes on the new shape," it's "stalls/times out live traffic on the largest table for however long the scan takes."

The re-validation is also somewhat wasted work: the prior `NO ACTION` constraint already guaranteed every existing `results.check_id` / `runs.suite_id` value is valid, but Postgres has no "trust me, skip the scan" flag for `ADD CONSTRAINT` — only `NOT VALID` + a separately-run `VALIDATE CONSTRAINT` gets you out from under the exclusive-scan lock.

**2. Constraint-name / naming-convention check — passes, no concern.** `backend/app/db/base.py` defines `NAMING_CONVENTION["fk"] = "fk_%(table_name)s_%(column_0_N_name)s_%(referred_table_name)s"`, and `923dab15eb8c_baseline.py` literally created these constraints as `fk_results_check_id_checks` (line 292) and `fk_runs_suite_id_suites` (line 200). The migration's `_FKS` list names match exactly — no drift.

**3. Model change is inert until the migration runs, and doesn't require a two-step — good.** `ForeignKey(..., ondelete="CASCADE")` in `models.py` is DDL metadata only; SQLAlchemy doesn't enforce cascade in Python. So there's no "new code needs new schema" ordering hazard either direction: old code + new schema behaves identically to old code + old schema except that a previously-failing `suite_service.delete_suite` (session.delete → ORM cascades `checks` via `delete-orphan`, but `results`/`runs` had no `ondelete`, so Postgres raised `ForeignKeyViolation` on any suite that had ever run) now **succeeds** instead of erroring — a strict widening of behavior, not a breaking change. Confirmed via `backend/app/services/suite_service.py:213-218` and `Suite.checks` cascade config (no ORM-level cascade to `Run`, so the fix is entirely DB-side).

### Rollback check
`downgrade()` is implemented (not `pass`) and correctly inverts the *schema* — drops the CASCADE constraints and recreates the original `NO ACTION` ones with matching names. It is honestly documented that it does **not** restore data: any rows cascade-deleted while the CASCADE constraint was live are gone on downgrade. The docstring calls this out explicitly and ties it to ADR 0020's accepted cascade-delete posture. This is an acceptable, well-documented limitation, not a defect — flagging for visibility only.

### Suggested approach (verify with whoever runs the deploy — I don't know current `results` row count / traffic pattern)
If `results` is still small (days of live prod data since 2026-06-28), the current single-transaction drop+add is probably fine in practice — but that's a size assumption, not something this review can confirm from the diff. The safer, deploy-agnostic shape to consider:

1. `ADD CONSTRAINT ... FOREIGN KEY (...) REFERENCES ... ON DELETE CASCADE NOT VALID` (fast — catalog-only, no scan, brief `ACCESS EXCLUSIVE`), *keeping* the old constraint dropped in the same or an adjacent statement.
2. A following statement/migration: `ALTER TABLE results VALIDATE CONSTRAINT fk_results_check_id_checks;` run **outside** the wrapped transaction (Alembic autocommit block, or its own non-transactional migration) — this only needs `SHARE UPDATE EXCLUSIVE`, which blocks other DDL but not concurrent reads/writes, while it does the same full scan in the background.
3. Same treatment for `fk_runs_suite_id_suites` on `runs`.

This can plausibly still ship as one PR/one release (unlike the drop-column two-step, there's no old-code-vs-new-schema incompatibility forcing a separate release) — just split into two statements/transactions so the validation scan doesn't ride the same lock as the drop. Confirm this against the actual `alembic upgrade head` execution path (the migrate ACA job) before deciding whether it's worth the extra complexity for current `results` volume.

### ✅ Verdict
`Conditional — 1 concern. Discuss with whoever runs the deploy.`

The FK-rename/naming/downgrade/rolling-deploy-compatibility checks all pass cleanly. The one open item is the `DROP CONSTRAINT` + `ADD CONSTRAINT` (full-scan validation) bundled into a single transaction against `results` — worth a size/traffic sanity check (and possibly the `NOT VALID`/`VALIDATE CONSTRAINT` split above) before this runs against the live Azure DB, since it will hold an effective `ACCESS EXCLUSIVE` lock on `results` for the scan's duration while old-code pods are still serving traffic.</result>

old code keeps working during a rolling deploy (the fix is a strict widening —
a previously-erroring delete now succeeds). **Tested up + down locally.**
Down restores the original NO ACTION constraints (rows deleted by a cascade
in the interim are gone, as accepted by ADR 0020).

Locking note (migration-safety review): the drop+add pair runs in one
transaction, so the ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock from DROP CONSTRAINT is held through
ADD CONSTRAINT's FK-validation scan of the referencing table. Accepted for the
current deploy: prod ``results``/``runs`` are days old (thousands of rows —
a milliseconds scan). If this pattern is ever repeated once ``results`` is
large, split it: ``ADD CONSTRAINT … NOT VALID`` (catalog-only) + a separate
non-transactional ``VALIDATE CONSTRAINT`` (SHARE UPDATE EXCLUSIVE — doesn't
block readers/writers).

@@ -257,6 +257,31 @@ def test_delete_cascades_to_checks(client: TestClient, db_session: Any) -> None:
     assert remaining == []  # cascade removed the child check
 
 
+def test_delete_succeeds_after_a_run_with_results(client: TestClient, db_session: Any) -> None:
+    """#540: results.check_id was the only FK without an ondelete, so deleting
+    a suite whose checks had RESULT rows hit fk_results_check_id_checks and
+    500'd — any suite that had ever run was undeletable. Found live (W7 smoke)."""
+    from backend.app.db.models import Result, Run
+
+    conn = _connection(db_session)
+    sid = client.post("/api/v1/suites", json=_payload(conn.id)).json()["id"]
+    check = Check(
+        suite_id=uuid.UUID(sid), name="row_count", expectation_type="expect_table_row_count"
+    )
+    db_session.add(check)
+    db_session.flush()
+    run = Run(suite_id=uuid.UUID(sid), status="succeeded", triggered_by="test:540")
+    db_session.add(run)
+    db_session.flush()
+    db_session.add(Result(run_id=run.id, check_id=check.id, status="pass"))
+    db_session.commit()
+    run_id = run.id  # capture before the cascade detaches the instance
+
+    assert client.delete(f"/api/v1/suites/{sid}").status_code == 204
+    db_session.expire_all()
+    assert db_session.scalars(select(Result).where(Result.run_id == run_id)).all() == []
+
+

from collections.abc import Sequence

from alembic import op

# revision identifiers, used by Alembic.
revision: str = "a9b0c1d2e3f4"
down_revision: str | None = "e5f6a7b8c9d0"
branch_labels: str | Sequence[str] | None = None
depends_on: str | Sequence[str] | None = None

# (constraint name, table, referred table, column)
_FKS = [
    ("fk_results_check_id_checks", "results", "checks", "check_id"),
    ("fk_runs_suite_id_suites", "runs", "suites", "suite_id"),
]


def upgrade() -> None:
    for name, table, referred, column in _FKS:
        op.drop_constraint(name, table, type_="foreignkey")
        op.create_foreign_key(name, table, referred, [column], ["id"], ondelete="CASCADE")


def downgrade() -> None:
    for name, table, referred, column in _FKS:
        op.drop_constraint(name, table, type_="foreignkey")
        op.create_foreign_key(name, table, referred, [column], ["id"])

results.check_id and runs.suite_id were the only FKs without an ondelete, so
DELETE /suites/{id} on any suite that had ever run tripped
fk_results_check_id_checks (checks cascade fired while runs->results rows
still referenced them) and 500'd. Found live by the W7 smoke cleanup.

- models.py: ondelete=CASCADE on both (ADR 0020's accepted cascade posture;
  matches every other FK in the schema).
- Migration a9b0c1d2e3f4: drop + recreate both constraints with the cascade.
  Backward-compatible (no column change; strict behavior widening). Tested
  up + down locally. Locking note in the docstring: drop+add share one
  transaction (ACCESS EXCLUSIVE through the validation scan) — fine at
  current table size, split NOT VALID/VALIDATE if ever repeated at scale.
- Regression test: create suite -> check -> run -> result, DELETE succeeds
  204 and results are gone.

Fixes #540

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>" && git push -qu origin fix/issue-540-results-check-fk-cascade 2>&1 | tail -1 && gh pr create --title "fix(db): suite delete cascades runs+results (Fixes #540)" --milestone "Week 7 — Deployment, hardening & docs" --body "## Summary
**Fixes #540** — found live during the Week-7 smoke cleanup: any suite that had **ever run** could not be deleted (500, \`fk_results_check_id_checks\` FK violation). \`results.check_id\` and \`runs.suite_id\` were the schema's only FKs without an \`ondelete\`.

- \`ondelete=\"CASCADE\"\` on both (ADR 0020's accepted cascade-delete posture; consistent with every other FK).
- Migration \`a9b0c1d2e3f4\`: drop + recreate the two constraints with the cascade. **Backward-compatible** (no column change — a previously-erroring delete now succeeds; old code unaffected). **Tested up + down locally.**
- Migration-safety review (agent) verdict: conditional-pass — the one concern (drop+add share one transaction ⇒ ACCESS EXCLUSIVE held through the FK validation scan) is accepted at current prod table size (days-old, O(10³) rows ⇒ ms scan) and documented in the docstring with the NOT VALID/VALIDATE split to use if the pattern ever repeats at scale.
- Regression test: suite → check → run → result → \`DELETE /suites/{id}\` returns 204 and results are gone.

## Rollback plan
\`alembic downgrade -1\` restores the original NO ACTION constraints (documented: rows cascade-deleted in the interim are not restored — ADR 0020).

## Test plan
- \`alembic upgrade head\` → \`downgrade -1\` → \`upgrade head\` clean locally.
- Full backend suite **1207 passed**, 96% cov; black/ruff/mypy green.
- Post-merge: Deploy #3 rolls the migrate job; live proof = deleting the two temp smoke suites that surfaced the bug.

content.text:
`low effort → 1 diff pass → no verify → ≤4 findings`

## Turn 1 — read

One tool call: read the unified diff (`git diff @{upstream}...HEAD; git diff HEAD`
to cover both committed and uncommitted changes, or `git diff main...HEAD` /
the target passed as an argument). Skip test/fixture
hunks (`test/`, `spec/`, `__tests__/`, `*_test.*`, `*.test.*`,
`fixtures/`, `testdata/`) — test-file changes are not reviewed at this level.
No subagents, no full-file reads.

## Turn 2 — findings

Flag runtime-correctness bugs visible from the hunk alone: inverted/wrong
condition, off-by-one, null/undefined deref where adjacent lines show the value
can be absent, removed guard, falsy-zero check, missing `await`,
wrong-variable copy-paste, error swallowed in a catch that should propagate.
Also flag — still from the hunk alone — new code that duplicates an existing
helper visible in the diff context, and dead code the diff leaves behind.

Do **not** flag style, naming, perf, missing tests, or anything outside the
hunk.

Output at most **4 findings**, most-severe first, one line each:
`path/to/file.ext:123 — what's wrong and the concrete failure`. If nothing
qualifies, output exactly `(none)`.

diff --git a/backend/alembic/versions/a9b0c1d2e3f4_results_check_fk_cascade.py b/backend/alembic/versions/a9b0c1d2e3f4_results_check_fk_cascade.py
new file mode 100644
index 00000000..104dc609
--- /dev/null
+++ b/backend/alembic/versions/a9b0c1d2e3f4_results_check_fk_cascade.py
@@ -0,0 +1,58 @@
+"""suite-delete cascades: results.check_id + runs.suite_id -> ON DELETE CASCADE (#540)
+
+Revision ID: a9b0c1d2e3f4
+Revises: e5f6a7b8c9d0
+Create Date: 2026-07-02 04:00:00.000000+00:00
+
+Deleting a suite that had ever RUN 500'd: the ORM cascades the suite's checks,
+but ``results.check_id`` (and, next in line, ``runs.suite_id``) had **no
+``ondelete``**, so the DB rejected the delete with a ForeignKeyViolation.
+Found by the Week-7 live smoke cleaning up its temp suites.
+
+CASCADE matches the schema's posture elsewhere (ADR 0020: cascade-delete
+accepted; ``results.run_id`` / ``checks.suite_id`` already cascade) and
+changes nothing about retention outside the delete path.
+
+**Backward-compatible**: constraint drop + recreate only, no column change;
+old code keeps working during a rolling deploy (the fix is a strict widening —
+a previously-erroring delete now succeeds). **Tested up + down locally.**
+Down restores the original NO ACTION constraints (rows deleted by a cascade
+in the interim are gone, as accepted by ADR 0020).
+
+Locking note (migration-safety review): the drop+add pair runs in one
+transaction, so the ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock from DROP CONSTRAINT is held through
+ADD CONSTRAINT's FK-validation scan of the referencing table. Accepted for the
+current deploy: prod ``results``/``runs`` are days old (thousands of rows —
+a milliseconds scan). If this pattern is ever repeated once ``results`` is
+large, split it: ``ADD CONSTRAINT … NOT VALID`` (catalog-only) + a separate
+non-transactional ``VALIDATE CONSTRAINT`` (SHARE UPDATE EXCLUSIVE — doesn't
+block readers/writers).
+"""
+
+from collections.abc import Sequence
+
+from alembic import op
+
+# revision identifiers, used by Alembic.
+revision: str = "a9b0c1d2e3f4"
+down_revision: str | None = "e5f6a7b8c9d0"
+branch_labels: str | Sequence[str] | None = None
+depends_on: str | Sequence[str] | None = None
+
+# (constraint name, table, referred table, column)
+_FKS = [
+    ("fk_results_check_id_checks", "results", "checks", "check_id"),
+    ("fk_runs_suite_id_suites", "runs", "suites", "suite_id"),
+]
+
+
+def upgrade() -> None:
+    for name, table, referred, column in _FKS:
+        op.drop_constraint(name, table, type_="foreignkey")
+        op.create_foreign_key(name, table, referred, [column], ["id"], ondelete="CASCADE")
+
+
+def downgrade() -> None:
+    for name, table, referred, column in _FKS:
+        op.drop_constraint(name, table, type_="foreignkey")
+        op.create_foreign_key(name, table, referred, [column], ["id"])
diff --git a/backend/app/db/models.py b/backend/app/db/models.py
index 7622ddf5..9a3829a1 100644
--- a/backend/app/db/models.py
+++ b/backend/app/db/models.py
@@ -328,8 +328,11 @@ class Run(Base):
     )
 
     id: Mapped[uuid.UUID] = _uuid_pk()
+    # CASCADE (#540): runs (and their results, via the run_id FK) die with the
+    # suite — ADR 0020's accepted cascade posture. Without it a suite that had
+    # ever run 500'd on delete.
     suite_id: Mapped[uuid.UUID] = mapped_column(
-        UUID(as_uuid=True), ForeignKey("suites.id"), nullable=False
+        UUID(as_uuid=True), ForeignKey("suites.id", ondelete="CASCADE"), nullable=False
     )
     status: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(16), nullable=False)
     triggered_by: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(String(256))
@@ -354,8 +357,11 @@ class Result(Base):
     run_id: Mapped[uuid.UUID] = mapped_column(
         UUID(as_uuid=True), ForeignKey("runs.id", ondelete="CASCADE"), nullable=False
     )
+    # CASCADE (#540): was the schema's only FK without an ondelete — a suite
+    # delete cascaded checks while runs→results rows still referenced them, so
+    # any suite that had ever run 500'd on delete (ADR 0020 accepts cascade).
     check_id: Mapped[uuid.UUID] = mapped_column(
-        UUID(as_uuid=True), ForeignKey("checks.id"), nullable=False
+        UUID(as_uuid=True), ForeignKey("checks.id", ondelete="CASCADE"), nullable=False
     )
     status: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(16), nullable=False)
     # SQL-aggregatable scalar the check measured + per-check runtime (ADR 0012).

import json, sys, urllib.request, urllib.parse
spa, scope = sys.argv[1], sys.argv[2]
p = "frontend/e2e-live/.auth/session.json"
d = json.load(open(p))
key = [k for k in d if k.startswith("oidc.user:")][0]
u = json.loads(d[key])
body = urllib.parse.urlencode({
    "grant_type": "refresh_token", "client_id": spa,
    "refresh_token": u["refresh_token"],
    "scope": f"{scope} openid profile",
}).encode()
req = urllib.request.Request(
    "https://login.microsoftonline.com/07b592a3-12ee-4631-abb1-8a7b73d91241/oauth2/v2.0/token",
    data=body,
    headers={"Origin": "https://dataq-app-frontend.purplefield-f7322a1b.westus2.azurecontainerapps.io",
             "Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded"})
try:
    resp = json.load(urllib.request.urlopen(req))
    u["access_token"] = resp["access_token"]
    if "refresh_token" in resp:
        u["refresh_token"] = resp["refresh_token"]
    d[key] = json.dumps(u)
    json.dump(d, open(p, "w"))
    print("refreshed ok, expires_in:", resp.get("expires_in"))
except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
    print("REFRESH FAILED:", e.code, e.read()[:300])
EOF

import json, sys, urllib.request, urllib.parse
spa, scope = sys.argv[1], sys.argv[2]

    fields = {"grant_type": "refresh_token", "client_id": spa, "refresh_token": u["refresh_token"]}

    req = urllib.request.Request(

        resp = json.load(urllib.request.urlopen(req))
        u["access_token"] = resp["access_token"]
        if "refresh_token" in resp: u["refresh_token"] = resp["refresh_token"]
        d[key] = json.dumps(u)
        json.dump(d, open(p, "w"))
        print("refreshed ok with scope:", sc, "expires_in:", resp.get("expires_in"))

EOF

Fixed **#393** (opencensus AzureLogHandler `lock=None` on Py3.13) en route. **GHCR package→repo connect done** (Actions-access grant → CI's `GITHUB_TOKEN` can push) and the **Deploy workflow validated end-to-end** (#403 fixed the migrate-command + frontend-pnpm bugs #401/#402; build→push→`alembic upgrade head`→ACA roll + SWA deploy all green on `v6`). **Post-deploy hardening (2026-06-28):** two production bugs surfaced and fixed — **#405** (Celery beat crashed on startup: the embedded `worker -B` beat re-nulled `self.lock` inside the opencensus `AzureLogHandler.createLock` fork on Python 3.13, silently killing ALL periodic tasks — orchestration polling, scheduled-suite dispatch, gap recovery, and sample-failure purge; fixed by making `createLock` idempotent in `backend/app/core/logging.py` + a network-free regression test; **#407** merged) and **#406** (deployed app couldn't read Key Vault: `AzureKeyVaultStore` called `DefaultAzureCredential()` with no args but the api+worker container runs a USER-assigned managed identity and `AZURE_CLIENT_ID` was unset — blocked connection tests, suite runs, AND orchestration polling; fixed by adding `AZURE_CLIENT_ID = azurerm_user_assigned_identity.app.client_id` to `local.app_env` in `deploy/terraform/azure/containerapps.tf`; **#408** merged). Backend image `:v7` built+pushed from main (with #405+#406); api+worker rolled to v7; App Insights re-enabled on the worker (the #405 mitigation of temporarily dropping `APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING` on the worker is reverted) — landed as **#409** (`image_tag` default v4→v7). **Orchestration polling is now live end-to-end** — beat starts clean (zero NoneType crashes) with App Insights on, api healthy (401, auth-enforced), ADF+Airflow connections polling via the 10-min beat fallback, Key Vault secrets read successfully. **Post-deploy feature batch (2026-06-29, all merged; prod image `:v7`→`:v10`):** Slack + email alert publishers behind the `ResultPublisher` seam (#413, `:v8`), column-aware failing-sample redaction (#417) + the #383/#384/#395/#423 hardening batch (`:v9`, bump #414), URL-encode DB password (#421/#395), always-alert operationally-failed runs (#419), alerting upsert race fix (#420/#384), per-run check outcome in the runs table (#425/#423), mypy gate over `backend/tests` (#418), and — **pulling ADR 0012 post-v1 Theme A forward — freshness & volume monitor-kinds end-to-end** (run engine #426 + authoring path & check-editor UI #437, `:v10`, bump #438). Prod frontend image `:v2`; URL `https://dataq-app-frontend.purplefield-f7322a1b.westus2.azurecontainerapps.io`. Follow-up **#512** (multi-arch frontend QEMU build was impractically slow/flaky — 45-min stall → cancel) **fixed in #518**: pin the frontend Dockerfile `build` stage to `--platform=$BUILDPLATFORM` so the arch-neutral Vite build runs natively once and only the per-arch nginx `serve` stage differs — frontend publish dropped from a 45-min stall to **~1min**, and a native-arm64 `:latest` multi-arch is published reliably again. AWS/GCP deploy IaC stays post-v1 (#505).

content.text:
The live-smoke session is complete — everything you asked for ran and verified, plus four production bugs it flushed out were fixed, deployed, and proven the same day.

## What ran and verified

**Live smoke (#531 lane, harness Flows A/B/C).** Signed in headed as Olivia (no `az` consent change needed — the lane's captured session + a refresh-token trick covered all API auth). Browser lane 3/3; `e2e_smoke.py` in bearer mode against prod. Flow A (Snowflake): three run modalities green — scheduled, Airflow-triggered, manual. Flow B (UC): green after fixing a run-path regression it caught. Flow C (flat-file in place): green. The standing Week-1 deferred live warehouse/file smoke is **discharged**.

**#525 spans.** Deployed to prod and confirmed in App Insights: request spans + linked `run_suite` task traces, joined to logs via `dataq.request_id`.

**#492 — Azure Monitor→ADF webhook: closed.** The Common Alert Schema carries no `runId`, so I implemented it as a poll-now signal (`AlertPing` → immediate targeted poll, PR #534), then wired the Action Group + `PipelineFailedRuns` metric alert on `dataq-harness-adf` and fired a deliberate failure (`pl_dataq_smoke_fail`, kept in the factory for re-smoking): **failure visible in DataQ 4m14s after fire**, the whole chain on one request_id. Under the 5-minute gate.

**MCP smoke.** All 4 canonical queries verified at protocol level against live `/mcp` (list_suites → get_suite_results → trigger_suite_run → get_adf_pipeline_status). For the client-side NL-selection check, point Claude Desktop at it with:
```json
{ "mcpServers": { "dataq": {
  "command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "mcp-remote@0.1",
    "https://dataq-app-frontend.purplefield-f7322a1b.westus2.azurecontainerapps.io/mcp"]
} } }
```

## What the smoke flushed out (all fixed, deployed, proven)

| Found | Fixed | Status |
|---|---|---|
| UC run-path dialect regression (#535) | #537 | UC re-run green |
| Databricks PAT leaked into traceback-frame locals in App Insights (#536) | #538 (no-locals + URL-userinfo scrub) | confirmed clean in prod logs |
| Suites that ever ran couldn't be deleted — bare FK (#540) | #542 (cascade migration, safety-reviewed) | both temp suites deleted live, 204 |
| Deploy workflow frontend step flaky (#539) | filed (2× az `KeyError` + 1× Docker Hub 502) | backend/migrate unaffected; manual retry works |

## Your action items
1. **Rotate the Databricks PAT** (exposed pre-#538): mint a new PAT → update KV secret `conn-unity-catalog-retail` → re-test the connection. Flagged in memory too.
2. #539 when convenient — add retry/backoff to the frontend deploy step (3/3 runs needed a manual retry, two different transients).
3. The last Week-7 externals: team onboarding, the irreversible KV purge-protection tfvars call, and the optional Claude Desktop NL check above.

Docs close-out PR [#543](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/543) (progress.md + CLAUDE.md status, Flow rows flipped to verified) auto-merges when CI goes green — a watcher is on it. Prod is on current main (backend `acd43a06` with all fixes; frontend on its last good image — no frontend code changed).
